title: Spiranthes coccinea
intro: Spiranthes coccinea, commonly known as the red lady's tresses, is a perennial orchid native to North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Spiranthes coccinea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Spiranthes coccinea is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Spiranthes coccinea is a species of orchid commonly known as the red-spotted lady's tresses. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae. The genus Spiranthes is a group of terrestrial orchids characterized by their spiral inflorescences and tuberous roots. Within the Spiranthes genus, S. coccinea is one of the species that can be found in North America. It is often classified as a member of the subgenus Spiranthes section Spiranthes.
uses: Spiranthes coccinea, also known as the red lady's slipper orchid, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help in treating wounds and skin conditions. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The roots are also believed to have antibacterial properties, making it potentially useful in treating infections. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of these medicinal uses.

These compounds have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. side_effects: Spiranthes coccinea, also known as the red spiral orchid, is not typically used as a medicinal plant. However, if ingested, it may cause gastrointestinal issues such as nausea and vomiting due to the presence of toxic compounds. In addition, handling the plant may cause skin irritation in some individuals. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can also cause allergic reactions in sensitive people.
